title: Building a Statistical Price-Level EA in MQL5
subtitle: Z-Score Entry/Exit from TP Distribution — Mean/Median/Mode/SD/Percentiles
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: MEDIUM EXTRACT — z-score as entry trigger (price far from mean = reversal), P25/P75 interquartile as S/R zone, KDE for mode detection. More relevant for ranging markets than trending. Skewness/kurtosis as distribution quality metrics.

core_concept.z_score_signal: z = (latest_TP - mean_TP) / std_TP; long if z <= -ZScoreSignalEnter; short if z >= +ZScoreSignalEnter
core_concept.z_score_exit: exit when |z| <= ZScoreSignalExit (price returns toward mean)

keep: Z-score as deviation gate: z > 2.0 = price far from mean = potential mean reversion
P25/P75 as statistical S/R zone — value area concept
KDE for mode detection (most common price) as fair value
Skewness/kurtosis as distribution quality metrics (for instrument calibration)
discard: Mean reversion strategy as primary — doesn't fit our trend-following approach
improve: Use P25/P75 as zone boundaries for 'value area' — demand zone entering below P25, supply above P75
Z-score > 2.0 at our supply zone = double confirmation (statistical overextension + structural zone)
Skewness: if skew > 0, distribution biased upward — weight BUY signals heavier
